title: Replace hand-rolled auth with timing-safe bcrypt flow
prompt:
A developer rolled their own authentication because `mix phx.gen.auth` 'was too much.' The result is a module with multiple issues: SHA-256 for hashing, variable-time `==` for verification, no user-not-found timing equalization, and the verify function leaks which case branch matched via flash messages. Rewrite the module to use `Bcrypt.hash_pwd_salt/1` for hashing, `Bcrypt.verify_pass/2` for verification, `Bcrypt.no_user_verify/0` in the missing-user branch, AND a single generic error message for all failure modes (so the server can't be used as an oracle for 'which emails exist'):

```elixir
defmodule MyApp.Accounts.Auth do
  def register(attrs) do
    hash = :crypto.hash(:sha256, attrs["password"]) |> Base.encode16()
    attrs = Map.put(attrs, "password_hash", hash)
    %MyApp.Accounts.User{} |> MyApp.Accounts.User.changeset(attrs) |> MyApp.Repo.insert()
  end

  def verify(email, password) do
    case MyApp.Repo.get_by(MyApp.Accounts.User, email: email) do
      nil -> {:error, "No such user"}
      user ->
        expected = :crypto.hash(:sha256, password) |> Base.encode16()
        if expected == user.password_hash do
          {:ok, user}
        else
          {:error, "Wrong password"}
        end
    end
  end
end
```

Write the fix to `lib/my_app/accounts/auth.ex`. The error message must be the SAME (`:invalid_credentials`) for both branches.
tier_rationale: Hard — four combined issues (hash, compare, timing equalization, error oracle). Each is individually known but combination is non-trivial.
